behaviors.description: A cell containing `=SUM(A1:A10)` evaluates to the correct sum of values in A1..A10.
behaviors.justification: Real failures often come from parser→range resolver→evaluator wiring (inclusive bounds, row iteration, type coercion), which a single-function unit test can miss.
behaviors.test_description: Create an in-memory workbook, set A1..A10 to numeric values, set target cell to `=SUM(A1:A10)`, recalc, and assert the computed value matches expected arithmetic sum.

behaviors.description: A cell referencing another formula cell (e.g., `=B1+1` where B1 is `=A1*2`) produces correct cascaded result.
behaviors.justification: This depends on dependency graph construction plus multi-cell evaluation order; mocking internals could hide propagation defects.
behaviors.test_description: Set A1 to a number, B1 to `=A1*2`, C1 to `=B1+1`, run recalculation, and assert B1/C1 values are correct from cascaded evaluation.

behaviors.description: Circular dependency (A1↔B1) is detected and returns circular reference error instead of looping.
behaviors.justification: Cycle detection failures usually appear at graph/evaluator interaction boundaries, not in isolated helper functions.
behaviors.test_description: Set A1 to `=B1` and B1 to `=A1`, trigger evaluation, assert both cells produce the engine’s circular-reference error and evaluation terminates.

behaviors.description: VLOOKUP exact match returns correct value; missing key returns error.
behaviors.justification: Correctness requires function implementation plus range parsing/indexing and error propagation; pure function mocks can miss table-addressing bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Populate a lookup table range, evaluate exact-match `VLOOKUP` for present and absent keys, and assert returned value vs not-found error code.

behaviors.justification: Nested IF correctness depends on expression parsing and branch evaluation semantics together, not only branch logic in isolation.
behaviors.test_description: Set A1 to values spanning branches (e.g., 5, 15, 25), evaluate `=IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, "high", "medium"), "low")`, and assert each expected label.

behaviors.description: CONCATENATE joins values with separator and skips empty cells.
behaviors.justification: Failure risk is in how function args are resolved from cell references and empties normalized, which can be hidden by direct unit mocks.
behaviors.test_description: Fill referenced cells with text and some empties, evaluate CONCATENATE formula with separators, and assert output omits empty-cell contributions.

behaviors.description: Cross-sheet reference (`=Sheet2!A1+1`) resolves correctly when sheet exists.
behaviors.justification: This tests workbook/sheet namespace resolution across modules; isolated unit tests may miss sheet-scoping defects.
behaviors.test_description: Create workbook with Sheet1 and Sheet2, set Sheet2!A1, place `=Sheet2!A1+1` in Sheet1, recalc, and assert expected result.

behaviors.description: When a referenced cell changes, dependents recalculate in correct topological order.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily dependency DAG invalidation/scheduling behavior across components; unit tests of individual nodes can miss stale-order bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Build a dependency chain/diamond, compute baseline, mutate an upstream cell, trigger incremental recalc, and assert all downstream values reflect the new source correctly.

behaviors.description: Undo after formula edit restores formula text and previously computed dependent values.
behaviors.justification: Requires history stack + formula storage + recalculation working together; mocking one layer can hide state-restore defects.
behaviors.test_description: Set up formulas with dependents, edit a formula and recalc, invoke undo, then assert restored formula text and all dependent computed values match pre-edit snapshot.

behaviors.description: Save to disk and reload preserves identical formulas/values with no floating-point precision change.
behaviors.justification: The real failure boundary is serializer/deserializer + filesystem persistence + parser reload path; in-memory mocks can miss binary-format round-trip issues.
behaviors.test_description: Create workbook with representative formulas and floating-point values, save to a temp file, reload, and assert formulas and numeric values match with strict/defined precision tolerance.
